title: Best of Atlanta Comedy Showcase
location: Laughing Skull Lounge, 878 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Weekly showcase featuring the city's top stand-up talent rotating through. Intimate 80-seat room with a full bar — perfect for couples who like quick laughs and craft cocktails. Friday night date-night classic.

title: From Afrique, With Love: A Photography Exhibition (Opening Night)
location: Old Rabbit Art Gallery, 309A Peters St SW, Atlanta, GA
description: Opening night of a month-long photo exhibition by two photographers who traveled through West Africa during the Year of Return. Gallery, gift shop, music, and rotating food vendors. A moody, romantic, culturally rich date.

title: Harland Williams at Center Stage
location: Center Stage Theater, 1374 W Peachtree St NW, Atlanta, GA
price: Ticketed (Live Nation) — check site for current price
description: Off-the-wall Canadian comedian (Dumb & Dumber, Half Baked) brings his absurd, physical comedy to Midtown. Great low-key date with a beer-and-laughs vibe.

title: Art of the Game — Opening Reception
location: Gallery Chimera, 624 N Highland Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Group exhibition timed to the FIFA World Cup featuring international artists exploring soccer, culture, and connection. Meet the artists, browse mixed-media works, and enjoy an intimate opening-night reception in Poncey-Highland.

title: Tropical Elegance Latin Night — Salsa & Bachata Lesson + Dancing
time: 8:00 PM - 2:00 AM (lesson 8-10 PM, party 10 PM-2 AM)
location: Brimstone Restaurant & Tavern, 10595 Old Alabama Rd Connector, Alpharetta, GA
description: Beginner-friendly salsa and bachata lesson followed by a full Latin dance social. Salsa, bachata, merengue, reggaeton. No experience needed — perfect excuse to hold each other close and learn a new skill together. Runs every Saturday.

title: Vegan Takeover at Kamayan Filipino Restaurant
location: Kamayan ATL, 5150 Buford Hwy NE A230, Doraville, GA
description: Pop-up vegan takeover at a Filipino restaurant on Buford Hwy. Great for adventurous foodie couples — explore plant-based Filipino flavors (kare-kare, lumpia, pancit) in a group hut setting.

title: Steph Tolev at Helium Comedy Club
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3073 Donald Lee Hollowell Pkwy NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Sharp, unapologetic stand-up from the Canadian comic on a national tour. Multiple showtimes make it easy to build a full date night around — dinner at Westside Provisions, then laughs.

title: Juneteenth Friday Jazz at the High Museum
location: High Museum of Art, 1280 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
price: VIP $40 members / $55 non-members (includes Amy Sherald exhibit access)
description: Special Juneteenth edition of the High's signature Friday Jazz series. Live music, art viewing, cocktails, and the city mingling on a long summer night. Quintessentially Atlanta date — art, jazz, and city views.

title: Reggae on the Rooftop Festival — Juneteenth Weekend
location: Air Roofrop (formerly Suite Lounge), 75 Luckie St NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Second annual rooftop reggae/dancehall/soca/Afrobeats festival with skyline views, complimentary jerk chicken and rum punch (while supplies last), DJs, indoor + outdoor two-level setup. 6 hours of Caribbean vibes — peak date energy.

title: Vegan Soul Sunday — Real Men Cook Vegan (Father's Day Edition)
location: New Life Presbyterian Church, Atlanta, GA
description: Father's Day edition of the beloved Vegan Soul Sunday cookout. Plant-based soul food, community, and good energy. Casual, foodie, family-friendly date — easy win for a relaxed Sunday afternoon.

title: Sabor Fino Latin Dance Social at Politan Row
location: Politan Row at Colony Square, 1197 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Late-night Latin dance social at Midtown's food hall. Bring your appetite, stay for the dancing. Couples who already know their salsa or want to learn — great vibe, food-and-drink options right there.

title: Plant to the Future: Food, Freedom & Black Futures Conference
location: Pittsburgh Yards | The Food Studio, Atlanta, GA
description: Day-long plant-based food conference with chefs, panels, tastings, and a community dinner. Inspiring, educational, and full of good food — ideal for a couple that geeks out on plant-forward culture and Black foodways.

title: Vinyl Sessions: June Edition
description: Curated vinyl-only listening night — the 12th installment. Intimate, music-nerd-romantic vibe: bring a date who likes talking about albums over drinks. A quieter, more thoughtful date alternative to loud clubs.
